title: Context window is Claude's working memory, not its knowledge

body.concept: The context window is all the text Claude can reference when generating a response — including the response itself. It is NOT the large corpus Claude was trained on. Think of it as a finite 'working memory' that holds the current conversation, system prompt, and tool results. Claude's max context window is up to 1M tokens.
body.analogy: Training data is like everything you learned in school over many years. The context window is like your desk: it can only hold so many papers at once. No matter how much you learned in school, you can only work with what's physically on your desk right now.
body.visual: A desk (context window) with limited surface area. Papers pile up as conversation turns grow. The bookshelf behind (training data) is vast but you can't read from it directly — only what's on the desk.
